target.name: Nova the Optimal AI + MIND Build Week candidate

included: Current source, release, install, browser-tour, and live-execution receipts
Stable six-scenario core index with sanitized public execution receipts
Three preserved historical negative TestForge reviews and the current complete-stage TestForge review
Evidence/traceability reconciliation completed after the current review
Hash-bound refreshed video production inputs and production-host doctor receipt
Hash-bound final local video render and automated verification receipt
Public YouTube publication, anonymous playability, bounded watch checks, and complete human viewing receipt
Public GitHub repository metadata and first-published revision parity receipt
Judge-facing content parity, anonymous repository/README readability, public-video-link presence, and release hash receipt
excluded: Live Devpost feedback/provenance entry, rights attestation, team acceptance, and Devpost submission
constraints: Claims are limited to named receipts and their recorded revisions.
A valid live execution is not a universal behavioral claim.
The final TestForge verdict reviewed the package's then-current INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE decision; it did not itself certify release readiness.
This manifest records the release operator's post-review reconciliation, not a second TestForge review.
safety_boundary: No publication, account mutation, destructive operation, or external action is inferred from local evidence alone; external actions require their own observed receipt.

observed.statement: The current shipping inventory records 201 directories, 443 files, and zero symlinks: MIND 147 and Nova 296.
The current whole-entry verifier passes with an empty generated-debris finding list.
The current release receipt and extracted-release validation bind the two archives; extraction validation is valid and its archive hashes match the release receipt and checksums.
observed.consequence: Current release archive structure and captured bytes.

observed.statement: After the preferred-name correction and debris cleanup, current-host refresh, 147/147 and 296/296 installed-cache equality, 28-handle discovery, and an initially empty temporary-home install all pass.
observed.consequence: Named install and discovery paths only; no model behavior inferred.

observed.statement: The browser tour receipt passed five layout viewports, seven activations, reduced-motion checks, and no-JavaScript assertions.

The stable core live-probe status is valid and binds SHA-256 values for six valid clean-host GPT-5.6-sol analyses: direct-negative, tour, Ludis character, MIND decision, Agentic orientation, and professional brief. Each analysis names a sanitized public execution receipt bound by SHA-256 to ignored local raw.
The professional brief uses the preferred lowercase professional name stunspot; MIND response normalization is transparently recorded.
A preserved pre-fix TestForge scenario executed validly but returned REVIEW_FAIL and INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE because its older sanitized stage omitted review artifacts.
observed.consequence: Historical negative review; neither a current product pass nor a plugin-defect finding.

observed.statement: The first complete-stage TestForge attempt executed validly but returned REVIEW_FAIL because cited receipt/helper files and an immutable stage revision were absent, while the host policy blocked independent helper execution.
observed.consequence: Historical negative review; its custody gaps were re-entry conditions.

observed.statement: The next TestForge attempt executed validly but returned REVIEW_FAIL because a one-review run overwrote the shared live index, creating a one-versus-six contradiction; it also required public execution receipts.
observed.consequence: Historical negative review; stable-core indexing and public raw receipts were re-entry conditions.

observed.statement: The final complete-stage TestForge review executed validly against a privacy-clean, Git-bound 513-file public stage and returned REVIEW_PASS_WITH_CONDITIONS for the package's own then-current INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE decision. It identified stale manifest/review state, obsolete Markdown traceability IDs, demo re-entry, and bounded residual risks; it did not certify release readiness.
observed.consequence: Evidence-quality review only; subsequent documentation reconciliation is separately recorded in this manifest.

observed.statement: GitHub reports Stunspot/nova-the-optimal-ai-mind as PUBLIC on main, and the first published remote revision matched the local candidate commit 57857aec41854c328200ac5f636d3962365e1251.
observed.consequence: Initial repository publication only; judge-facing content parity and anonymous readability are established separately by C-OBS-015 and E-015.

observed.statement: The hash-bound 172.066667-second 1080p local final MP4 and matching 43-cue SRT passed 27 of 27 automated checks and three independent quality gates; stunspot confirmed the complete watch without reporting a defect.
observed.consequence: Local render and complete human viewing only; publication and submission require separate evidence.

observed.statement: The exact final filename is public at https://youtu.be/1cqEFrP6FZw. Anonymous playability returned OK with 1080p video and audio formats; opening, midpoint, and final-card visual checks passed with burned captions.
observed.consequence: Public video and bounded playback only; Devpost submission remains separate.

observed.statement: Judge-facing content commit 8e0f1f8fcb97dad43d975cc8b394a60af97b1b74 matched local HEAD and origin/main. Anonymous repository and raw-README requests returned HTTP 200, the README response contained the exact public-video ID, and both release ZIP hashes matched their recorded values.
observed.consequence: Exact judge-facing content, anonymous HTTP readability, video-link presence, and release hash readback only; Devpost and legal/team gates remain separate.

unresolved.statement: Live Devpost feedback/provenance entry, rights, team acceptance, and Devpost submission are complete.

impact_map: Current release validation is distinct from installed-host validation.
A valid live scenario establishes only its named oracle.
Historical negative reviews remain audit evidence and are not current product failures.
The final conditional review assessed evidence quality, not external contest completion.
Initial repository metadata and judge-facing content parity/readability are distinct observations; the remaining contest gates remain external.
Local render verification, complete human viewing, public upload, anonymous playability, and Devpost submission are distinct states with separate receipts.

invariants.statement: The current release archives match their current receipt and extracted-release validation.
Current installation claims require post-correction evidence against the cleaned 147/296 source trees.
The tour satisfies its named browser checks.
The direct-negative scenario does not load a forbidden specialist.
Historical TestForge negatives are not represented as current product passes or plugin-defect findings; the current review is represented with its exact conditional verdict and limits.
Public-repository claims distinguish the initial publication receipt from the judge-facing content parity, anonymous-readability, link-presence, and release-hash receipt.
No repository or video receipt is represented as rights, live Devpost feedback entry, team, or submission proof.
A local final-render claim requires the exact MP4/SRT hashes and a passing bounded verification receipt.
A public-video claim requires an observed URL, public playability, matching final filename, video and audio stream availability, and bounded playback checks.

risks.statement: Current release payload diverges from its release receipt or contains generated debris.
The corrected, cleaned source/release is not the payload installed or discovered by the host.
The browser tour regresses in its named layout or activation checks.
Simple direct work spuriously activates a forbidden specialist.
Live routing or TestForge review evidence is overstated.
Public repository metadata, judge-facing content parity/readability, or release hash readback is absent or misrepresented.
Live Devpost feedback entry, rights, team, or Devpost gates are absent or misrepresented.
The final local video render diverges from canonical inputs or fails bounded media and quality checks.
The public video is absent, private, wrong, or lacks playable video/audio evidence.

scenarios.title: Current static and release extraction validation
preconditions: Current source, release receipt, and archives exist.
action: Validate current tree and extracted archives against current receipt/checksums.
expected: Tree is clean; both current archives validate and match.

scenarios.title: Post-correction cleaned-tree install evidence
preconditions: Corrected and cleaned source/current release are available.
action: Refresh current selectors, compare caches, inspect discovery, and repeat initially empty-home install.
expected: Current source and installed payloads match at 147/296; clean host discovers 28 handles.

scenarios.title: Tour browser checks
expected: Five layout viewports, seven activations, and named accessibility assertions pass.

scenarios.title: Stable six-scenario core live execution index
preconditions: Core bound status and sanitized execution receipts exist.
action: Inspect status, six SHA-256-bound analyses, and their public execution receipts.
expected: Six valid named executions with one common privacy-clean staged tree and no TestForge self-review entry.
forbidden: Allowing a single TestForge run to overwrite the core index.

scenarios.title: Complete-stage TestForge evidence review
preconditions: Current evidence documents and receipts are staged.
action: Run independent TestForge review against the current complete stage, then reconcile any identified documentary conditions without representing that remediation as a second review.
expected: Reviewer returns an evidence-bound verdict and separates residual risk from external authority gates.
forbidden: Treating historical negative reviews as current product results.
Treating post-review reconciliation as reviewer recertification.

scenarios.title: Initial public repository observation
preconditions: GitHub repository and local candidate revision exist.
action: Inspect GitHub visibility/default branch and compare the first published remote revision with local HEAD.
expected: Repository reports PUBLIC on main and first-published local/remote revisions match.
forbidden: Inferring final-head parity or signed-out readability.

scenarios.title: Judge-facing public repository content verification
preconditions: Final documentation and public-video-link content commit exists on origin/main.
action: Compare local HEAD with origin/main, read both release archive hashes, and fetch the repository page and raw README anonymously.
expected: Content commit parity passes; repository page and raw README return HTTP 200; README contains the exact public-video ID; release hashes match their receipts.
forbidden: Inferring Devpost, rights, or team state from repository access.

scenarios.title: Final local video render verification
preconditions: Canonical production inputs and final MP4/SRT exist.
action: Verify exact artifact hashes, duration, streams, caption custody, and bounded quality checks.
expected: The under-three-minute 1080p MP4 and matching SRT pass all recorded automated and independent gates.
forbidden: Treating render mechanics alone as publication or submission evidence.

scenarios.title: Public YouTube publication and playback verification
preconditions: Final video is uploaded to YouTube and its URL is available.
action: Inspect Studio filename/visibility, query anonymous playability and stream formats, spot-check opening/midpoint/final card, and record the complete human watch.
expected: The exact final filename is public, anonymously playable with video/audio streams, visually intact at three checkpoints, and completely viewed by stunspot.
forbidden: Inferring Devpost submission from video publication.

scenarios.title: Remaining contest account and submission gates
action: Confirm live Devpost feedback/provenance entry, rights, team, and Devpost gates.
expected: Truthful receipt or URL per gate.
forbidden: Inferring Devpost or legal/team completion from repository or video receipts.

tests.note: Preserved historical negative review; not a current acceptance test.
Preserved historical negative review; not a current acceptance test.
Preserved historical negative review; not a current acceptance test.
Review executed validly; verdict is REVIEW_PASS_WITH_CONDITIONS for the preceding INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE decision, not an independent release-ready certification.

command: bounded final-video render and verification pipeline
YouTube Studio observation, anonymous player-response check, and bounded watch-UI spot check
git HEAD/origin parity, release SHA-256 readback, and anonymous GitHub repository/raw-README HTTP checks

findings.statement: The preserved pre-fix TestForge review assessed an older sanitized stage that omitted review artifacts.
findings.next_action: Retain for audit; it is superseded by the current complete-stage review.

findings.statement: Successive TestForge attempts exposed missing staged custody and then an index orchestration contradiction; independent helper execution remained blocked by nested host policy.
findings.next_action: Retain historical reviews; current stage supplied stable core, public receipts, Git binding, and documentary reconciliation. Keep nested-host execution limit as residual risk.

findings.statement: Live Devpost feedback/provenance entry, rights, team acceptance, and Devpost remain external human gates.
findings.next_action: Authorized representative records each remaining external action.

residual_risks.statement: The original six clean-host core runs are content-hash-bound but not Git-revision-bound.
residual_risks.treatment: Limit claims to their named prompts, recorded hashes, model, and receipts; do not infer immutable-revision provenance for those six runs.
residual_risks.revisit_condition: Broader rerun is staged and Git-bound.

residual_risks.statement: Sanitized public receipts bind ignored local raw through SHA-256, but external reviewers cannot independently compare the private originals.
residual_risks.treatment: State the public/private evidence boundary; do not claim public raw is the original record.
residual_risks.revisit_condition: A shareable independently auditable raw-evidence policy is authorized.

residual_risks.statement: Nested host policy blocked independent reruns of verification scripts, Git state, and archive hashes during the final review.
residual_risks.treatment: Treat supplied receipts as reviewed evidence, not independently re-executed proof.
residual_risks.revisit_condition: A review host with the necessary read/write execution authority is available.

residual_risks.statement: Six core prompts and one TestForge review prompt are narrow samples, not a routing or quality census.
residual_risks.revisit_condition: A broader ecological evaluation is completed.

residual_risks.statement: Live Devpost feedback/provenance entry, rights, team, and Devpost submission remain external gates.
residual_risks.treatment: Keep human-gated; require observed receipts and authorized attestations.
residual_risks.revisit_condition: External receipts or URLs are available.

review.reviewer: TestForge operator plus verification reviewer in one clean-host GPT-5.6-sol scenario
review.verdict_scope: The review passed the package's own then-current INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE decision with conditions; it did not certify release readiness.

conditions.statement: Markdown traceability IDs must match manifest IDs.
Demo must remain explicitly re-entry-gated until its stale content is refreshed and separately verified.
conditions.disposition: remediated_through_local_render, complete human viewing, and public playback receipts

review.remaining_limit: The final review was not repeated after documentation reconciliation. Its exact verdict and residual risks remain controlling evidence boundaries.

basis: Current package, static, install, tour, and six named live-execution gates are green within their stated boundaries.
The final complete-stage TestForge review executed validly and returned REVIEW_PASS_WITH_CONDITIONS for the preceding INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE decision.
Manifest and Markdown traceability conditions have been reconciled without representing that work as a second TestForge review.
The exact under-three-minute local MP4 and matching SRT passed bounded render verification; stunspot completed the full watch and the exact filename is public with bounded playback evidence.
GitHub public-repository metadata, judge-facing content parity, anonymous repository/README readability, public-video-link presence, and release hash readback are observed.
All remaining local limits and external gates are explicit.
not_a_claim_of: Independent rerun under the reviewer host.
Live Devpost feedback/provenance entry, rights, team acceptance, or Devpost submission.
authority_required: Authorized representative for live Devpost feedback/provenance entry, rights, team, and Devpost actions.
